h2h_summary: England lead the only previous head-to-head meeting, a 6-1 demolition in the 2018 World Cup group stage, though Panama's Felipe Baloy did score his country's first-ever World Cup goal in that match.

notable_results: 6-1 England win - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 2018

team_style_home: Panama set up in a compact 4-2-3-1 or 5-4-1 defensive shape out of possession, relying on collective organisation, hard running, and quick vertical counter-attacks when chances arise, with Carrasquilla as the creative link and set-pieces as a key weapon.
team_style_away: Under Thomas Tuchel, England play a possession-dominant 4-2-3-1 that morphs into a 3-2-5 in build-up, with high-intensity pressing, inverted wingers, and over 70% average possession recorded throughout qualifying.
wc_history_home: Panama made their World Cup debut at Russia 2018, losing all three group games to Belgium, England, and Tunisia but earning a place in history when Felipe Baloy scored the country's first-ever World Cup goal; 2026 is only their second appearance.
wc_history_away: England won the 1966 World Cup on home soil — their only major international title — and have since reached the semi-finals in 1990 and 2018, arriving at 2026 ranked 4th in the world seeking a second world title.
group_context: Group L features England, Croatia, Ghana, and Panama; England and Croatia are the heavy favourites to advance, making the Panama vs England match — the final group game — potentially decisive for England's qualification path and essentially Panama's last chance to avoid a group-stage exit.
strengths_home: Disciplined defensive organisation capable of frustrating higher-ranked opponents
Dangerous set-piece threat and physical presence in both penalty areas
Counter-attacking pace and directness with Waterman and Diaz able to punish high defensive lines
strengths_away: World-class attacking trio in Kane, Bellingham, and Saka capable of dismantling organised defences
High-press system that suffocates teams with limited technical quality in build-up
Dominant possession game with structural flexibility under Tuchel that can adapt to any opponent
